roadmap_and_sfi_path.highlight: Draft roadmap created: EIP draft → specs/tests (EL+CL) → client impl → devnet → testnet → fork; ecosystem tasks include RPC updates and decryptor standards
Execution spec PR open (vibe-coded with Claude, 14k lines, based on Fusaka+FOCIL branch); very early draft, needs review
No CL spec contributor yet; Loring/Anthony to reach out to consensus client teams, potentially with a funded bounty
EL implementation planned in Besu; Erigon expressed interest and has its own CL — strong candidate for parallel impl

frame_transactions_and_design.highlight: Frame transactions enable two-signature enforcement (user + builder), preventing key-leaking attacks not addressable via slashing alone
Gottfried: ticket signature (not decrypted tx) is the hard part — needs post-quantum sig support and unlinkable sender for true front-running protection
Correct Lucid usage requires sending ticket from a one-time address unlinkable to real sender; account abstraction/frame transactions could greatly improve this UX

eip_criticism_and_design_honesty.highlight: Gottfried: at least one security-critical bug found in draft EIP (easy fix); consensus rule changes not yet fleshed out; marketing should not oversell Lucid as a complete solution
Gottfried: 'future-proof agnosticism' claim is cryptographically incorrect — in an encrypted mempool, agnosticism gives adversary best-of-all options; should be toned down
Lucid is best available design given current tech, but a purpose-built threshold IBE design would require starting from scratch

coalition_and_funding.highlight: Encrypted Mempool Coalition launching tomorrow with ~20 partners (incl. Besu, Erigon, Fairblocks, Web3 Privacy); website at encryptedmempool.org
$6k confirmed from DAO Security Fund QF round; Octant Epoch 12 and Shutter Champions Melee 2 (~$10k) pending; social bounties proposed as dual engineering+marketing signal
Bounty strategy: funding specific milestones (e.g., CL spec) signals community demand to core devs, not just pays for work

action_items.action: Loring/Anthony to reach out to consensus client teams (esp. Erigon) to recruit CL spec contributor, potentially with bounty incentive
Justin to create GitHub issue for next working group call so Anthony can link it from coalition website
Gottfried to review coalition webpage and flag any claims to tone down before tomorrow's launch

decisions.decision: Lucid PM project to be hosted in Justin's GitHub repo; Yannick and others added as collaborators for roadmap artifacts
Initial $6k to be used primarily for marketing/bounties rather than direct engineering; social bounty model preferred to demonstrate community demand

targets.target: Encrypted Mempool Coalition website + tweet launch — June 11, 2026 (tomorrow)
